sentence: Subjective:

HPI: The patient, Alexis, is a [NOT MENTIONED] who has been experiencing numbness and tingling in her right hand for approximately six months, with the symptoms becoming more persistent over the last month.
She reports experiencing pain, particularly when typing on a keyboard or using a mouse.
The numbness and tingling are constant, whereas the pain is intermittent.
She has found some relief with rest and the use of ibuprofen, specifically 600mg four times a day.
Past medical history: Alexis's past medical history is not mentioned in the transcript.
Review of systems: The patient reports no pain at night that wakes her up, and her symptoms are not affecting her sleep.
Current medications: I prescribed ibuprofen 600mg four times a day for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure, pulse rate, oxygen saturation, and temperature are not mentioned in the transcript.
Physical exam: The patient has pain on extension and flexion of the wrist, and has a positive Tinel's sign.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I suspect that Alexis has carpal tunnel syndrome.
Problem list: Carpal tunnel syndrome; unknown.
Plan:
I would like to put Alexis in a splint to wear at night to help alleviate symptoms.
I will continue to prescribe ibuprofen 600mg four times a day for pain management.
I will also order an EMG and nerve conduction study to further evaluate the nerve impingement in her wrist.
We will start with conservative treatment and reassess after the test results.
Follow-up:
I will follow up with Alexis after the EMG and nerve conduction study to discuss the results and determine the next course of action, which may include physical therapy or surgery.
